_comment: Single source of truth for quantitative model benchmark figures. Update this file when simulation parameters change — dashboard_updater will inject these values into the HTML automatically.

supply_chain._source: Python Monte Carlo N=5000 | 8 suppliers | supplier-specific MTBF (health-score weighted) | recovery 5mo | USD 9B revenue at risk
supply_chain.rev_at_risk_derivation_note: MO-07 2026-06-07: USD 9B = USD 7.9B single-source spend × 1.139 product-revenue multiplier. COO/VP Supply Chain to validate multiplier against product P&L. Conservative upper bound.

ebitda._source: EBITDA Stress Monte Carlo N=5000 | vol=12% | 96% cost ratio | USD 57B revenue | FX cost pass-through: USD 680M unhedged JPY/KRW COGS exposure | M-05 implemented 2026-05-28

cov006_stress_scenario.note: Bad debt provision at 1.44% vs 0.80% threshold — incremental charge ~USD 365M reduces EBITDA from 2,280M to 1,915M, below COV001 floor of 2,128M. P(breach) recomputation required.

ebitda.mo06_note: MO-06 2026-06-07: Revenue FX unhedged USD 3,400M (FX001+FX002) added to EBITDA model. Current demand_var does not capture USD appreciation impact on CNY/EUR revenue streams. Rerun Monte Carlo with revenue_fx_unhedged included in revenue distribution.
ebitda.covenant_breach_computation_note: RECOMPUTED 2026-06-07: 44.2% (interim) → 69.4% incorporating MO-01 (COV006 -365M EBITDA) and MO-06 (rev FX variance). Method 1 sensitivity estimate; range 64.5%-76.9%. Full Monte Carlo rerun to follow per CFO/Quant deadline 2026-06-20.

unified_fx_model._description: MO-06/MO-03 2026-06-07: Unified FX exposure model design. Both Hedge Analyser and EBITDA Stress model must derive all FX inputs from treasury_positions.csv as single source of truth. Eliminates structural divergence discovered by panel (MO-03, MO-06).

architecture.step_1_load: Read treasury_positions.csv — all positions, all dates
architecture.step_2_split: Split by asset_type: FX positions → hedge model; Revenue/Cost FX → EBITDA model
architecture.step_4_validate: Assert SUM matches model_benchmarks.json totals; log discrepancy if diff > 0

verification_2026_06_07.verdict: Panel MO-03 arithmetic error — CSV data correct at USD 9,140M. No data change required.

unified_fx_model.implementation_note: dashboard_updater.py should call load_fx_positions(treasury_positions.csv) once and pass results to both HedgeModel.compute_var() and EBITDAModel.compute_p_breach(). Never hardcode gross totals in either model.

fx_hedge._source: Hedge Analyser Monte Carlo | FX (Revenue+Cost): 9% vol | Commodity (DRAM+NAND): 32% vol | M-03 panel recommendation implemented 2026-05-28
fx_hedge.var_95_unhedged_note: 1303 = gross VaR without hedge; 1050 = net VaR after hedge; improvement = 253 (MO-02 corrected 2026-06-07)

model_fx_revenue_reconciliation._description: HIGH-07 2026-06-07: Reconciliation table for FX-exposed revenue across three models.

supply_chain_model.definition: Total revenue at risk from supply chain disruption (NOT FX exposure). Equals single-source spend (7.9B) × revenue multiplier (1.139). Represents production revenue loss if critical suppliers fail — a supply risk metric, not an FX metric.

ebitda_stress_model.definition: Unhedged FX COST exposure — JPY/KRW denominated COGS not covered by hedges. FX003 unhedged (980-600=380M) + FX004 unhedged (620-320=300M) = 680M. Represents COGS sensitivity to USD/JPY and USD/KRW movements.

hedge_analyser_model.definition: Gross FX REVENUE exposure — CNY/EUR denominated revenue before hedging. FX001 gross (3,200M) + FX002 gross (2,800M) = 6,000M. Represents revenue sensitivity to USD/CNY and USD/EUR movements.

model_fx_revenue_reconciliation.reconciliation_summary: No overlap or double-counting between the three figures — they measure distinct risk types: (1) supply chain operational loss, (2) COGS FX sensitivity, (3) revenue FX sensitivity. Board materials should present all three with clear labels. Combined FX exposure = 6,000M revenue FX + 1,600M cost FX gross = 7,600M total FX gross — consistent with treasury_positions.csv and model_benchmarks.fx_hedge.fx_gross_usd_m.
